title: Airport SMS Link
scenario: Before your flight, you get an SMS saying you must re-verify your boarding pass and pay a small fee using a link.

answers.A: Tap the link and pay the fee to board.
answers.B: Forward the SMS to a friend for advice.
answers.C: Open the airline app/website and check your boarding pass there.
answers.D: Call the airline using the number from their official website.

explanation: SMS payment/verification links are frequently used for phishing and scam fees. Verify in official apps or by calling official contact numbers.
proTip: Don’t pay or enter information from messages with urgency or fees.
